memoryEntries.title: 2026-03-24 — Monday
memoryEntries.summary: Mission Control shifted from a visual rebuild into a usable local MVP with persisted operating data.
content: Visibility keeps beating aesthetics. The dashboard only becomes trusted when actions, blockers, and ownership are obvious.
The local MVP should stay deliberately simple: one shared model, one persisted file, and enough CRUD to run the workflow by hand.
Approvals deserve their own screen because that is the clearest CEO exception queue surface.

memoryEntries.title: 2026-03-22 — Saturday
memoryEntries.summary: The command layer should filter noise rather than simply mirror every event from underlying tools.
content: Daniel needs concise exceptions, not another busy chat feed.
Any polished UI still has to answer: what matters now, who owns it, and what is blocked?

agents.name: Henry
agents.summary: Routes work, shields the CEO from noise, and decides what deserves escalation.

agents.name: Charlie
agents.summary: Owns automations, event routing, and the plumbing behind reliable ops.

agents.name: Scout
agents.summary: Scans fast for hooks, angles, and market movement.

agents.name: Quill
agents.summary: Turns raw direction into hooks, scripts, captions, and persuasive assets.

agents.name: Codex
agents.summary: Implements product changes and keeps the local system shipping.

agents.name: Manager
agents.summary: Checks capacity, handoffs, and execution balance.

approvals.title: Approve homepage CTA copy refresh
approvals.detail: Publish a stronger consultation CTA and trust-proof block on the Refined homepage.

approvals.title: Approve Friday council move
approvals.detail: Shift the weekly review block one hour later to avoid overlap with a clinic handoff.

approvals.title: Approve local MVP scope cut
approvals.detail: Defer live Discord and Telegram syncing until after the local CRUD MVP is stable.

events.message: Queued a homepage copy approval for Daniel.
Relay schema draft updated for future Discord and Telegram sync.
Mission Control local MVP work resumed from the UI rebuild baseline.

longTermNotes: Workflow visibility beats aesthetics, but the aesthetics still need to communicate confidence.
Mission Control should become the canonical shared state, with Discord and Telegram acting as surfaces on top.
CEO mode is exceptions only: decisions, blockers, approvals, and high-risk actions.
